response.justification: The candidate comfortably clears the minimum bar — a CS degree and roughly 25 years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems — and brings genuine entrepreneurial experience as a long-running open-source platform founder. However, this is a director-level AI strategy role, and his track record is that of a strong senior/staff builder rather than a recognized AI leader: his AI exposure (MCP code generators, agent tooling at re:cinq) is recent and infrastructure-adjacent, with no demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale, no advanced AI degree, and no evidence of executive-level strategy, customer advisory, or organizational influence.
strengths: 25+ years of broad, hands-on engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Founder of GISCollective, a long-lived open-source geo-data platform, showing entrepreneurial drive and product ownership
Current work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, plus a history of community teaching, workshops, and conference talks
concerns: No recognized AI expertise or demonstrated AI-powered products/platforms; AI experience is recent and tooling-focused
Career trajectory tops out at senior/staff IC with no evidence of director-level scope, strategy work, or advising executive stakeholders
No master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and limited depth in cloud platform strategy beyond project-level AWS/Kubernetes use

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and shipped AI products
Deep and broad engineering experience exceeding the 15-year minimum
Missing director-level strategic leadership and customer advisory experience
